title: Malty Silky Argentinian Lardons Empanadas
description: A festive and delicious empanada recipe featuring malty flavors and silky texture, made with plant-based-friendly lardons-render and cider vinegar.

ingredients.name: Lardons-render
ingredients.notes: Plant-based-friendly, made with olive oil and herbs.

ingredients.name: Malted beer brew
ingredients.notes: Adds a malty, slightly sweet flavor.

instructions: Mix together the flour, lardons-render, cider vinegar, malted syrup, dried oregano, garlic powder, and salt in a large bowl until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 10 inch thick.
Brush the edges of each circle with water and place a spoonful of filling in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Cook the empanadas for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Serve the empanadas hot with your choice of toppings.
Serve with chopped cilantro, sliced salsa, and crumbled feta cheese if desired.
